spec: food-safe silicone, 6 mm OD x 4 mm ID
description: Food-safe silicone tubing. The default flexible tube for water + low-pressure liquids. Stretches to grip 6 mm hose barbs.
notes: FDA-grade silicone is widely available; verify supplier specifies 'food-safe' if dispensing into consumables. Lifetime in peristaltic pumps: ~6 months under continuous use.

spec: food-safe silicone, 8 mm OD x 5 mm ID
description: Slightly larger silicone tubing for higher-flow water lines. Pairs with 1/4" / 6 mm barb fittings on the upper end.
Clear vinyl (PVC) tubing. The aquarium-store default. Cheaper than silicone but less heat-tolerant and slightly stiffer.
notes: Not food-safe. Prefer silicone where heat or food contact applies.

spec: PTFE / Teflon, 4 mm OD x 2 mm ID
description: PTFE (Teflon) tubing. Chemically inert — handles aggressive solvents and high-temp fluids that ruin silicone.
notes: Stiffer than silicone — needs compression fittings, not hose barbs. The 4×2 size is the de-facto Bowden-tube spec for E3D / Mosquito hotends.

spec: polyurethane (PU/PUN), 6 mm OD x 4 mm ID
description: Polyurethane pneumatic tubing. Standard for push-fit fittings on industrial / DIY pneumatics. Returns to shape after kinks.
notes: OD must match the push-fit collet exactly — 6 mm push-fit accepts 6 mm OD only, not 5 mm or 6.35 mm.
